    "R1.2": "The section makes several inferential moves that warrant consistency checks: the claim that the model-radiologist accuracy gap is 'consistent with prior studies' is asserted without qualification; the aggregation strategy (mean across reviewers) is stated but its suitability relative to the ordinal Likert scale is not justified; and the attribution of weak clinical-utility agreement to 'differing rating thresholds' is a causal claim that needs logical grounding.",
    "R1.3": "Concrete numeric claims are present (factual accuracy 3.8 vs 4.2; alpha 0.41–0.58) but no table, figure, or citation backs them inline. The assertion that the gap replicates 'several prior studies on transformer-based report generation systems' is unsupported by any reference, making evidence–claim alignment a central concern.",
    "R1.6": "All three paragraphs consist almost entirely of run-on sentences chained with coordinating conjunctions ('and … and … and').   },
  "clarification_needed": true,

rationale.R1.2: The section makes several inferential moves that warrant consistency checks: the claim that the model-radiologist accuracy gap is 'consistent with prior studies' is asserted without qualification; the aggregation strategy (mean across reviewers) is stated but its suitability relative to the ordinal Likert scale is not justified; and the attribution of weak clinical-utility agreement to 'differing rating thresholds' is a causal claim that needs logical grounding.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete numeric claims are present (factual accuracy 3.8 vs 4.2; alpha 0.41–0.58) but no table, figure, or citation backs them inline. The assertion that the gap replicates 'several prior studies on transformer-based report generation systems' is unsupported by any reference, making evidence–claim alignment a central concern.
rationale.R1.6: All three paragraphs consist almost entirely of run-on sentences chained with coordinating conjunctions ('and … and … and'). Sentence boundaries, paragraph cohesion, and verb-tense consistency (present vs. past) need substantial editing; this is the most immediately visible deficiency in the text.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this review primarily intended to assess the methodological soundness and empirical claims of the section (R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing), or to improve the prose quality and sentence structure (R1.6 load-bearing)?
